### Chrome DevTools MCP smoke stage (per skill-rules.md Rule 3 + Rule 8)

The story touches `web/src/` (single file: `web/src/components/v-timeline-scrubber.ts` line 398 — the bypass fix). Per the persistent-facts policy the MCP smoke stage is REQUIRED. Story 6.3's lead-side smoke at `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/6-3-smoke-evidence/lead-smoke-summary.md` covers the AC6 four-step sequence; this QA stage's MCP-equivalent role is satisfied by the **Playwright L4 fixture** (AC4) which fires `page.emulateMedia({ reducedMotion: 'reduce' })` against a real Chromium runtime — that is the canonical reduced-motion verification surface per ADR-0010 (Playwright is the CI-time tier; the new fixture is exactly that).

**Proposed agent-time MCP supplement (optional, for a future Epic 6 cross-review):**

| Step | Tool | Asserted observation | AC |
|---|---|---|---|
| (a) | `emulate` → `reduced-motion: reduce` + `navigate_page` → `/c/v1-jupiter` | `<v-timeline-scrubber>` chapter-marker tooltip CSS resolves `transition: opacity 0ms ease` (the `--v-duration-fast` token at 0) | AC2 |
| (b) | `evaluate_script` → `getComputedStyle(document.documentElement).getPropertyValue('--v-duration-fast').trim()` | Returns `"0ms"` (the `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override at `:root` is reactive) | AC1, AC5 |
| (c) | `evaluate_script` → `window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches` | Returns `true` | AC5 |
| (d) | `press_key` H + `take_screenshot` ~50 ms later | HUD already at opacity 0 — no mid-fade frame | AC2 |
| (e) | `list_console_messages` | No errors beyond the Lit dev-mode banner | hygiene |

Smoke evidence path (if invoked): `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/6-3-smoke-evidence/mcp-<step>.png`. NOT required for Story 6.3 closure — the Playwright fixture is the binding gate per ADR-0010.

## Generated Tests — New QA-generated coverage

The dev stage shipped 1 new vitest file (`web/tests/reduced-motion-defense.test.ts` with 8 tests across 3 describe blocks) and 1 new Playwright spec (`web/tests/visual/reduced-motion-regression.spec.ts` with 5 baselines). The QA stage **extends the existing defense file with a 4th describe block (4 new tests)** to cover audit-doc integrity from angles orthogonal to the dev's three primary gates.

### tests/reduced-motion-defense.test.ts § 5 (4 new tests) — audit-doc integrity

Addresses Focus Area #2 from the parent orchestrator prompt: grep-verify (a) audit doc cites only existing files, (b) bypass-fix citation has not silently regressed, (c) JS-side canonical pattern files exist + carry the documented type, (d) belt-and-braces bare-literal sweep for the two most common bypass property shapes.

- **§ 5 (a) — § 2 surface inventory file existence.** Extracts every backticked `web/src/...:line` citation from the audit doc and asserts each path resolves under repo root. 19 unique citations checked; all resolve. Catches refactor-rename drift in the audit table.
- **§ 5 (b) — § 6 bypass-fix content integrity.** Reads `web/src/components/v-timeline-scrubber.ts` directly and asserts the tooltip rule uses `transition: opacity var(--v-duration-fast)` (NOT a bare millisecond literal). Asserts the audit doc § 6 names the file + the canonical token. Catches a silent revert of the Story 6.3 bypass fix.
- **§ 5 (c) — § 5 JS-side pattern canonical files.** Asserts the four files named in audit doc § 5.2 (`turn-choreography.ts`, `composite-layer.ts`, `view-frame.ts`, `voyager-camera-controller.ts`) exist AND each defines a `ReducedMotion(?:Probe|Source)` type per the documented pattern. Catches a rename of the DI type or a move of the canonical files.
- **§ 5 (d) — belt-and-braces bare-literal sweep.** Tighter grep specifically for `transition: opacity <N>ms` and `transition: transform <N>ms` two-property declarations — the most common bypass shape in the Epic 1–6 codebase (Story 6.3's actual bypass was exactly this shape). Defense-in-depth alongside the dev's § 2 broad sweep.

**Verification:** `cd web && npx vitest run tests/reduced-motion-defense.test.ts` → 12/12 pass (8 dev + 4 QA).

### No new Playwright file added

The dev's `web/tests/visual/reduced-motion-regression.spec.ts` already exercises 5 final-state baselines deterministic across 3 QA runs. No additional Playwright spec was warranted.

## QA Focus Area outcomes (per parent orchestrator prompt)

### Focus #1 — Pre-existing build-dist-layout flake investigation

**Outcome: NOT a flake — deterministic regression.** Root cause identified and filed as MED in `deferred-work.md` § "Story 6.3 (QA, 2026-05-24)".

Three consecutive runs of `npx vitest run tests/build-dist-layout.test.ts`:

| Run | Result | Tests | Failure |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | FAIL | 5 passed / 1 failed | `observed 0 violations, expected ≥3` |
| 2 | FAIL | 5 passed / 1 failed | `observed 0 violations, expected ≥3` |
| 3 | FAIL | 5 passed / 1 failed | `observed 0 violations, expected ≥3` |

**Root cause:** Story 6.2 (commit `d3b4311`, "feat(story-6.2): HUD dismiss/restore + narrow-viewport compaction + marker clustering" — Epic 5 retro Action item #8) added explicit `var(--v-edge-margin, 16px)` fallbacks to all four `<v-hud>` corner CSS rules. These rules live in `<v-hud>`'s Shadow-DOM `<style>` block — NOT in the external `main-*.css` bundle. When the synthetic test at `build-dist-layout.test.ts:405-548` intercepts the root HTML and strips the `<link rel="stylesheet">` tag, the shadow-DOM rule still applies and the `16px` fallback keeps each corner properly positioned. Result: top-right.right > VIEWPORT_WIDTH - 200, bottom-*.bottom > VIEWPORT_HEIGHT - 100 still all pass — violation counter stays at 0.

The Story 6.2 dev intentionally introduced this defense (paired with `web/tests/v-hud-corner-defensive.test.ts`). The Story 6.0 synthetic test's premise (no CSS link ⇒ corners collapse to (0,0)) is now over-specified — the defenses are not in conflict on intent, but the Story 6.0 test's specific failure-mode assumption no longer holds.

**Resolution:** filed as `[6.0/6.2 → 6.4 / MED]` in `deferred-work.md`. Three resolution options documented (retire the synthetic case; strip the `--v-edge-margin` token in the intercept; re-target on light-DOM consequence like `<v-chapter-copy>`). Routing: Story 6.4 (next Epic 6 story) or a dedicated maintenance commit.

**NOT auto-resolved in this QA pass** because the right shape requires a maintainer design decision — Story 6.3 is primarily AUDIT + DOCUMENTATION; rewriting an unrelated story's regression test is out of scope.

### Focus #2 — Audit-doc integrity defense extension

**Outcome: DELIVERED.** 4 new tests in `web/tests/reduced-motion-defense.test.ts § 5` (see "Generated Tests" above). Tests cover (a) file-existence of every audit citation, (b) bypass-fix content integrity, (c) JS-side canonical-file pattern, (d) belt-and-braces two-property bare-literal sweep.

### Focus #3 — Playwright fixture determinism

**Outcome: 5/5 baselines pass deterministic across 3 consecutive runs.** No flake. No deferred-work entry needed.

### Focus #4 — JS-side animation reduced-motion compliance spot-check

**Spot-check target: `web/src/chapters/pale-blue-dot/turn-choreography.ts`.**

**Source verification:**
- Lines 119-130 declare `ReducedMotionProbe` type and `defaultReducedMotionProbe` factory using `window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches`.
- The DI-friendly pattern guards against `typeof window === 'undefined'` and `typeof window.matchMedia !== 'function'` (Node test environment safe).
- The probe is consumed by the SLERP transition engine — when reduced-motion is active the SLERP is bypassed and the override snaps to the new aim instantly (per the module's own header docstring lines 47-51).

**Test coverage verification:**
- `web/src/chapters/pale-blue-dot/turn-choreography.test.ts` line 12: AC4/T2.4 reduced-motion path test marker present.
- Line 288: `describe('Story 5.2 AC4 — TurnChoreography reduced-motion path', ...)`.
- Line 289: `it('reduced-motion=true: substate transitions snap instantly (no SLERP)', ...)` — exercises the reduced-motion code path via the DI stub.
- Line 317: `it('reducedMotion probe uses window.matchMedia by default', ...)` — exercises the default probe (happy-dom matchMedia returns matches=false).

**Conclusion:** dev claim verified. `turn-choreography.ts` correctly probes matchMedia AND has unit-test coverage for both branches. By extension, the audit doc § 5.2's claim that all four canonical files follow the same pattern is plausible; the QA-added § 5 (c) test gates against drift on the other three.

### Focus #5 — Cross-component token verification (`--v-duration-fast` exists)

**Outcome: VERIFIED.** `web/src/styles/tokens.css:87` declares `--v-duration-fast: 120ms` inside the `:root` block (lines 6-106). Sibling tokens `--v-duration-base: 200ms` (line 88) and `--v-duration-slow: 400ms` (line 89) also confirmed. The reduced-motion `@media` override at `web/src/styles/global.css:46-52` zeroes all three. The Story 6.3 bypass fix at `v-timeline-scrubber.ts:398` (now :405 after the explanatory comment block) references `var(--v-duration-fast)` which resolves correctly.

No typo. The token exists. The dev's § 1 gate in `reduced-motion-defense.test.ts` independently pins this.

## Next Steps

- **Story 6.4 / maintenance commit:** address the `build-dist-layout.test.ts > catches a missing CSS-link regression (synthetic BUG-E5-007)` failure per the three resolution options documented at `deferred-work.md § Story 6.3 (QA, 2026-05-24)` item 1. Recommended: option 3 (re-target on light-DOM consequence) most faithfully preserves the test's original regression-coverage intent; option 1 (retire) is the lowest-risk delete if no other layout-collapse defense surfaces have regressed since Story 6.0.
- **Future stories adding animated surfaces:** consult `docs/accessibility/reduced-motion.md` § 8 "Forward-coupled obligations" — every new surface must add a row to § 2's table, use one of the `--v-duration-*` tokens or `ReducedMotion*` DI types, and re-run both `reduced-motion-defense.test.ts` and the Playwright L4 fixture.

## Decisions

- Filed pre-existing `build-dist-layout` failure as MED (not LOW) in deferred-work because the test is now deterministically wrong post-Story-6.2, not flaky. MED captures "fix required before next defense regression test gets misled into thinking everything is fine"; LOW would have under-weighted the urgency.
- Extended existing `reduced-motion-defense.test.ts` rather than creating a sibling file. The 4 new gates are coherent with the existing 3 describe blocks (same imports, same constants, same audit-doc target) and co-location keeps the reduced-motion defense surface single-source.
- Did NOT auto-resolve the synthetic BUG-E5-007 case because the right resolution shape requires a maintainer design choice between three reasonable options.
- Did NOT add a Chrome DevTools MCP smoke pass because the Playwright L4 fixture (`reduced-motion-regression.spec.ts`) already covers the canonical reduced-motion verification surface per ADR-0010, and Story 6.3 is primarily audit/documentation rather than user-facing surface introduction. The proposed MCP supplement is documented for an optional future cross-review.

## Issues Encountered

- Pre-existing `build-dist-layout.test.ts > catches a missing CSS-link regression (synthetic BUG-E5-007)` failure confirmed deterministic on `epic6` HEAD (NOT a flake — 3/3 runs failed identically with `observed 0 violations, expected ≥3`). Root-caused to Story 6.2 commit `d3b4311`'s defensive `var(--v-edge-margin, 16px)` fallback added to `<v-hud>` shadow-DOM corner CSS, which prevents the collapse pattern the Story 6.0 synthetic case was designed to detect. Filed as MED in `deferred-work.md` for Story 6.4 or maintenance commit; not Story-6.3-introduced.
